title: 1 Bedroom Apartments for Rent in Ohio City
metaTitle: 1 Bedroom Apartments for Rent in Ohio City, OH
metaDescription: 1 bedroom apartments for rent in Ohio City, Cleveland near the West Side Market and RTA Red Line. See rent ranges and voucher-friendly homes. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: 1 bedroom apartments for rent ohio city
secondaryKeywords: one bedroom rentals near west side market
answerBox: Ohio City, in Cleveland's near-west side ZIP 44113, has 1-bedroom apartments in converted Victorian-era rowhouses and newer buildings near the West Side Market and the RTA Red Line's Ohio City station. Citywide 1-bedroom rents run about $1,195 to $1,451 a month (Zumper/RentCafe, July 2026), and we work with voucher-friendly homes in the area.

body: Ohio City sits on Cleveland's near-west side in **ZIP code 44113**, directly across the Cuyahoga River from downtown. The neighborhood is anchored by the historic **West Side Market** and served directly by GCRTA's **Red Line at the Ohio City station**. A large share of the housing stock is late-1800s Victorian houses and rowhouses that have been divided into multi-unit buildings over the decades, and 1-bedroom units are among the most common configurations that come out of those conversions, alongside a smaller number of purpose-built apartment buildings closer to the market district.

A 1-bedroom in Ohio City gives a renter a separate bedroom and living space without the size — or price — of a full two-bedroom unit, which is one reason the configuration is in steady demand this close to downtown. We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Ohio City — [book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current 1-bedroom availability, or let our leasing team know your budget and move-in timeline.

sections.h2: How much does a 1-bedroom apartment cost in Ohio City?
body: Per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, Cleveland's citywide median 1-bedroom rent is **$1,195/month**. RentCafe (Yardi)'s July 2, 2026 report, which tracks larger professionally managed apartment buildings averaging about 690 square feet for a 1-bedroom, shows a higher citywide average of **$1,451/month**. Ohio City's proximity to downtown, the West Side Market, and the Red Line tends to put its 1-bedroom pricing at or above these citywide benchmarks, especially in newer construction, while conversions in older rowhouses can come in closer to the citywide median.

sections.h2: Getting around from a 1-bedroom in Ohio City
body: Ohio City has its own rapid-transit stop, the **Ohio City station on RTA's Red Line**, running to downtown Tower City in one direction and toward Cleveland Hopkins Airport and the east side, including University Circle, in the other. West 25th Street is the main commercial corridor, passing the West Side Market, Market Square Park, and the Hingetown area, with I-90 access nearby for anyone driving in or out of the neighborhood.

items: **ZIP code:** 44113, near-west side, across the river from downtown
**Transit:** RTA Red Line, Ohio City station; I-90 access nearby
**Landmarks:** West Side Market, Market Square Park, W. 25th Street corridor, Hingetown
**Housing stock:** Late-1800s houses and rowhouses converted into 1-bedroom units, plus newer apartment construction

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers for a 1-bedroom in Ohio City
body: The **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)** runs the Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program countywide, including Ohio City, through a year-round preliminary application with mini-lottery selection. Acceptance of a voucher for any specific Ohio City apartment still comes down to the individual landlord, since Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law and Cleveland has not enacted a local ordinance requiring landlords to accept vouchers. Every rental home we work with in and around Ohio City accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the CMHA process generally works.

sections.h2: Finding a 1-bedroom apartment in Ohio City
body: [Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current 1-bedroom availability in and around Ohio City with our leasing team. If you want more room, compare against our [2-bedroom Ohio City listings](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/2-bedroom-apartments-for-rent-ohio-city-cleveland/), or if a smaller footprint works, look at [studio apartments in Ohio City](/cleveland-rentals/apartments/studio-apartments-for-rent-ohio-city-cleveland/). For the full neighborhood-by-neighborhood picture, browse [apartments across Cleveland](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/). Once you've found a fit, [apply online](/apply).

Our application process reviews income relative to rent, rental history, and any voucher or co-signer support you can bring — consistent whether the unit is a 1-bedroom in Ohio City or elsewhere in our Cleveland-area selection. Bringing pay stubs, landlord references, or voucher paperwork to your showing can help speed up a decision.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with 1-bedroom apartments in Ohio City?
faq.a: Yes. We work with voucher-friendly rental homes in and around Ohio City as part of our broader Cleveland-area selection. Book a free showing to see current 1-bedroom availability, since specific units change regularly.

faq.q: How much does a 1-bedroom apartment cost in Ohio City?
faq.a: Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts the median 1-bedroom rent at about $1,195/month, while RentCafe's same-period report for larger managed buildings shows an average closer to $1,451. Ohio City, near downtown and the West Side Market, often prices at or above these citywide figures.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Ohio City in?
faq.a: Ohio City is in ZIP code 44113, on Cleveland's near-west side, directly across the Cuyahoga River from downtown.

faq.q: What transit is near 1-bedroom apartments in Ohio City?
faq.a: Ohio City has its own RTA Red Line station, connecting to downtown Tower City in one direction and Cleveland Hopkins Airport and the east side in the other. West 25th Street and I-90 are both close by.

faq.q: Do Ohio City landlords accept Section 8 vouchers for 1-bedroom units?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord. Cleveland, including Ohio City, has no ordinance requiring voucher acceptance, since Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law and the city hasn't passed its own. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with in the area accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready.
